parameters.name: lagmethod
parameters.label: Method
parameters.description: The method by which lag is applied to the channels.

parameters.name: lagunit
parameters.label: Lag Unit
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: overshootunit
parameters.label: Overshoot Unit
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: clamp
parameters.label: Clamp Slope
parameters.description: Clamps the slope (or velocity) to lie between the values listed in Max Slope below. Slope is expressed as value/Units.

parameters.name: aclamp
parameters.label: Clamp Acceleration
parameters.description: Clamps the acceleration to lie between the values listed in Max Acceleration below. Acceleration is expressed as value/(Units*2) .

parameters.name: lagsamples
parameters.label: Lag per Sample
parameters.description: Applies the lag to each sample of the channel instead of across the whole channel. Useful for working with multi-sample channels.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: When On resets (bypasses) the lag effect.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: overshoot2
parameters.label: Overshoot2
parameters.description: Applies overshoot to a channel. The first value is for overshoot while moving up, and the second is for overshoot while moving down.

parameters.name: accel2
parameters.label: Accel2
parameters.description: The first value limits the acceleration when it is rising, and the second value limits the acceleration when it is decreasing.

parameters.name: lag2
parameters.label: Lag2
parameters.description: Applies a lag to a channel. The first value is for lagging up, and the second is for lagging down. It is approximately the time that the output follows 90% of a change to the input.

parameters.name: slope2
parameters.label: Slope2
parameters.description: The first value limits the slope when it is rising, and the second value limits the slope when it is decreasing.

examples.label: Configure snap and Time Slice
examples.code:
n = op('lag1')
n.par.snap = False
n.par.timeslice = True
n.par.lag1 = 0
examples.description: Common settings from 25 real Derivative examples

examples.label: Alternative configuration
examples.code:
n = op('lag1')
n.par.snap = False
n.par.timeslice = True
